description: Evaluates Docker container isolation, network sandboxing, and resource limits for secure OpenClaw deployments

evals.name: basic-docker-isolation
evals.prompt: Set up Docker isolation for my OpenClaw project called 'acme-agent' in personal mode
evals.expected_output: Hardened Dockerfile with multi-stage build, non-root user, pinned base image; docker-compose.yml with security_opt, cap_drop ALL, read_only, mem_limit, cpus, pids_limit; internal network with ports bound to 127.0.0.1
expectations: Generates multi-stage Dockerfile with non-root user (UID 1001)
Produces docker-compose.yml with no-new-privileges and cap_drop ALL
Sets mem_limit to 1024m and cpus to 1.0 for personal mode
Creates internal bridge network with internal: true
Binds ports to 127.0.0.1 only

evals.name: network-egress-allowlist
evals.prompt: My OpenClaw agent needs to call the Claude API and GitHub API. Set up network isolation with egress controls for project 'dev-assistant'
evals.expected_output: Internal Docker network plus egress proxy configuration with Squid allowlist for anthropic.com and github.com; iptables rules or proxy-based filtering; agent container on internal-only network routing through proxy
expectations: Creates internal network for the agent container
Configures egress proxy (Squid or equivalent) with domain allowlist
Allowlist includes anthropic.com and github.com
Agent container has HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY environment variables
Agent container is NOT on the egress network directly

evals.name: team-deployment-isolation
evals.prompt: Containerize our team OpenClaw deployment 'platform-agents' with --team mode. We have 3 agent roles that need inter-container communication but restricted internet access
evals.expected_output: Multi-container docker-compose.yml with team-mode resource limits (2GB RAM, 2 CPUs, 200 PIDs per container), shared internal network for inter-container communication, egress filtering, per-container security hardening
expectations: Applies team-mode resource limits: 2048m memory, 2.0 CPUs, 200 PIDs
Creates shared internal network for inter-container communication
Each container has security_opt, cap_drop, read_only
Egress rules or proxy documented for controlled internet access
Separate service definitions for each agent role

evals.name: audit-existing-compose
evals.prompt:
Audit this docker-compose.yml for my OpenClaw deployment and harden it:

version: '3.9'
services:
  agent:
    image: node:latest
    privileged: true
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - /home/user:/host-home
    ports:
      - '3000:3000'
    environment:
      - API_KEY=sk-ant-12345
evals.expected_output: Security audit report identifying all issues: unpinned image, privileged mode, Docker socket mount, home directory mount, port on 0.0.0.0, inline secret; hardened replacement with all issues resolved
expectations: Flags node:latest as unpinned and bloated
Flags privileged: true as critical vulnerability
Flags Docker socket mount as critical
Flags home directory mount as sensitive path exposure
Flags port binding to 0.0.0.0 and inline API_KEY
Produces hardened replacement with all issues resolved

evals.name: false-positive-general-docker
evals.prompt: Help me create a Docker container for my Node.js web application with Express and MongoDB
evals.expected_output: This is a general Docker request, not an OpenClaw/agent isolation request. Should suggest general Docker best practices or redirect to appropriate skill, not invoke the full OpenClaw isolation workflow
expectations: Recognizes this is NOT an OpenClaw or agent isolation request
Does not apply the full 4-section OpenClaw isolation process
May offer general Docker guidance or redirect to a more appropriate skill
Does not generate egress proxy or agent-specific network isolation

evals.name: edge-case-wsl2-windows
evals.prompt: I'm on Windows 11 with WSL2 and Docker Desktop. Set up container isolation for my personal OpenClaw project 'win-agent'. My project is at C:\Users\me\projects\openclaw
evals.expected_output: Standard isolation configuration adapted for WSL2: warns about Windows path bind mount performance, recommends Linux filesystem paths, notes iptables rules apply inside WSL2 VM, adjusts .wslconfig memory guidance, generates compose with WSL2-compatible paths
expectations: Detects WSL2 environment and applies WSL2-specific guidance
Warns about /mnt/c/ bind mount performance degradation
Recommends copying project to Linux filesystem (~/projects/)
Notes iptables rules must be set inside WSL2 VM
Generates valid docker-compose.yml with all hardening applied
